name: standards-refresh.sh re-scrapes and re-parses a source purely on its numeric fetch interval picking up upstream changes with grandfathered epochs and no force flag
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r46-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/out && printf '

Encrypt data at rest

All data MUST be encrypted at rest.

' > $OUT/s.html && cat > $OUT/reg.yaml <e1.err && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/standards-refresh.sh" --registry $OUT/reg.yaml --now 2026-07-11T00:10:00Z --out-dir $OUT/out 2>e2.err; printf '

Encrypt data at rest

All data MUST be encrypted at rest.

Rotate credentials quarterly

All credentials MUST rotate on a fixed schedule.

' > $OUT/s.html && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/standards-refresh.sh" --registry $OUT/reg.yaml --now 2026-07-11T00:31:00Z --out-dir $OUT/out 2>e3.err; grep -q 'WROTE sid=r46-src' e1.err && grep -q 'SKIP sid=r46-src (not due; freq=30m)' e2.err && grep -q 'WROTE sid=r46-src' e3.err && grep -q 'rotate-credentials-quarterly' $T && grep -A12 'encrypt-data-at-rest' $T | grep -q "introduced_in: '2026-07-11T00:00:00Z'" && grep -A12 'rotate-credentials-quarterly' $T | grep -q "introduced_in: '2026-07-11T00:31:00Z'" && echo interval-driven-rescrape-reparses 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
